type BetaCompactionBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Content stringо‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

EncryptedContent stringо‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

Type Compactionо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

From [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

Role Assistantо‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

StopDetails [BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

Category BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryо‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryCyber BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "cyber"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryBio BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "bio"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryReasoningExtraction BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Explanation stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

FallbackCreditToken stringо‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

FallbackHasPrefillClaim boolо‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

RecommendedModel stringо‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

Type Refusalо‚љ

оЂЄ

StopReason [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

const BetaStopReasonEndTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "end\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonMaxTokens [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "max\_tokens"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonStopSequence [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "stop\_sequence"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonToolUse [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "tool\_use"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonPauseTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "pause\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonCompaction [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "compaction"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonRefusal [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "refusal"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonModelContextWindowExceeded [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

StopSequence stringо‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

Type Messageо‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

Usage [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InferenceGeo stringо‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

Iterations [BetaIterationsUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

OutputTokensDetails [BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

ThinkingTokens int64о‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

Content [][BetaContentBlockUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оЃ–

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оЃ–

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оЃ–

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaTextBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndCharIndex int64о‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Text stringо‚љ

Type Textо‚љ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationCharLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndCharIndex int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartCharIndex int64о‚љ

Type CharLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationPageLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndPageNumber int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartPageNumber int64о‚љ

Type PageLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Text stringо‚љ

Type Textо‚љ

IsError boolо‚љ

ToolUseID stringо‚љ

Type MCPToolResultо‚љ

type BetaCompactionBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Content stringо‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

EncryptedContent stringо‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

Type Compactionо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

From [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

Model Modelо‚љ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

Explains why the prompt cache could not fully reuse the prefix from the request identified by `diagnostics.previous_message_id`. `null` means diagnosis is still pending вЂ” the response was serialized before the background comparison completed.

const ModelClaudeSonnet4\_20250514 Model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"о‚љ

High-performance model with extended thinking

const ModelClaude\_3\_Haiku\_20240307 Model = "claude-3-haiku-20240307"о‚љ

Fast and cost-effective model

stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

Role Assistantо‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

StopDetails [BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

Category BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryо‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryCyber BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "cyber"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryBio BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "bio"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryReasoningExtraction BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Explanation stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

FallbackCreditToken stringо‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

FallbackHasPrefillClaim boolо‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

RecommendedModel stringо‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

Type Refusalо‚љ

оЂЄ

StopReason [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

const BetaStopReasonEndTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "end\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonMaxTokens [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "max\_tokens"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonStopSequence [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "stop\_sequence"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonToolUse [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "tool\_use"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonPauseTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "pause\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonCompaction [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "compaction"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonRefusal [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "refusal"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonModelContextWindowExceeded [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

StopSequence stringо‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

Type Messageо‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

Usage [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InferenceGeo stringо‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

Iterations [BetaIterationsUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

OutputTokensDetails [BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

ThinkingTokens int64о‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оЂЄ

The number of server tool requests.

WebFetchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web fetch tool requests.

The number of web search tool requests.

оЂЄ

StopDetails [BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

Category BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryо‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryCyber BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "cyber"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryBio BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "bio"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryReasoningExtraction BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Explanation stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

FallbackCreditToken stringо‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

FallbackHasPrefillClaim boolо‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

RecommendedModel stringо‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

Type Refusalо‚љ

оЂЄ

StopReason [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)о‚љ

One of the following:

const BetaStopReasonMaxTokens [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "max\_tokens"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonRefusal [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "refusal"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonModelContextWindowExceeded [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

StopSequence stringо‚љ

Type MessageDeltaо‚љ

оЂЄ

Usage [BetaMessageDeltaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The cumulative number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The cumulative number of input tokens read from the cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The cumulative number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

Iterations [BetaIterationsUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

type BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

OutputTokensDetails [BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

ThinkingTokens int64о‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оЂЄ

The number of web fetch tool requests.

WebSearchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web search tool requests.

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Text stringо‚љ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

type BetaCompactionBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Content stringо‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

EncryptedContent stringо‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

Type Compactionо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

From [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

Model Modelо‚љ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type Model stringо‚љ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Type CitationsDeltaо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaThinkingDelta struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

EstimatedTokens int64о‚љ

Per-frame increment of a coarse, running estimate of the tokens this thinking block has produced so far. Present whenever the `thinking-token-count-2026-05-13` beta is set; `null` unless `thinking.display` resolves to `"omitted"` and a count is due this frame. Sum the increments across `thinking_delta` frames on this block for a progress indicator. Each increment is a non-negative multiple of a fixed quantum and the cadence is rate-limited, so this is a deliberately lossy display hint, not a billable count; `usage.output_tokens` remains authoritative.

Thinking stringо‚љ

Type ThinkingDeltaо‚љ

оЂЄ

Type SignatureDeltaо‚љ

type BetaRefusalStopDetails struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

Category BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryо‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryCyber BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "cyber"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryBio BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "bio"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryReasoningExtraction BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Explanation stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

FallbackCreditToken stringо‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

FallbackHasPrefillClaim boolо‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

RecommendedModel stringо‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

Type Refusalо‚љ

оЂЄ

Name BetaServerToolUseBlockParamNameо‚љ

One of the following:

const BetaServerToolUseBlockParamNameAdvisor BetaServerToolUseBlockParamName = "advisor"о‚љ

оЂЄ

CacheControl [BetaCacheControlEphemeral](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

Type Ephemeralо‚љ

оЂЄ

TTL BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLо‚љOptional

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL5m BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "5m"о‚љ

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL1h BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "1h"о‚љ

type BetaTextBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationCharLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndCharIndex int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartCharIndex int64о‚љ

Type CharLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationPageLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndPageNumber int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartPageNumber int64о‚љ

Type PageLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Text stringо‚љ

Type Textо‚љ

type BetaThinkingConfigAdaptive struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Type Adaptiveо‚љ

оЂЄ

Display BetaThinkingConfigAdaptiveDisplayо‚љOptional

Controls how thinking content appears in the response. When set to `summarized`, thinking is returned normally. When set to `omitted`, thinking content is redacted but a signature is returned for multi-turn continuity. Defaults to `summarized`.

One of the following:

const BetaThinkingConfigAdaptiveDisplaySummarized BetaThinkingConfigAdaptiveDisplay = "summarized"о‚љ

const BetaThinkingConfigAdaptiveDisplayOmitted BetaThinkingConfigAdaptiveDisplay = "omitted"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Type Disabledо‚љ

type BetaThinkingConfigEnabled struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

BudgetTokens int64о‚љ

Determines how many tokens Claude can use for its internal reasoning process. Larger budgets can enable more thorough analysis for complex problems, improving response quality.

Must be в‰Ґ1024 and less than `max_tokens`.

See [extended thinking](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking) for details.

minimum1024

Type Enabledо‚љ

оЂЄ

Display BetaThinkingConfigEnabledDisplayо‚љOptional

Controls how thinking content appears in the response. When set to `summarized`, thinking is returned normally. When set to `omitted`, thinking content is redacted but a signature is returned for multi-turn continuity. Defaults to `summarized`.

One of the following:

const BetaThinkingConfigEnabledDisplaySummarized BetaThinkingConfigEnabledDisplay = "summarized"о‚љ

const BetaThinkingConfigEnabledDisplayOmitted BetaThinkingConfigEnabledDisplay = "omitted"о‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaThinkingConfigParamUnionResp interface{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Configuration for enabling Claude's extended thinking.

When enabled, responses include `thinking` content blocks showing Claude's thinking process before the final answer. Requires a minimum budget of 1,024 tokens and counts towards your `max_tokens` limit.

See [extended thinking](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking) for details.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaThinkingDelta struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

EstimatedTokens int64о‚љ

Per-frame increment of a coarse, running estimate of the tokens this thinking block has produced so far. Present whenever the `thinking-token-count-2026-05-13` beta is set; `null` unless `thinking.display` resolves to `"omitted"` and a count is due this frame. Sum the increments across `thinking_delta` frames on this block for a progress indicator. Each increment is a non-negative multiple of a fixed quantum and the cadence is rate-limited, so this is a deliberately lossy display hint, not a billable count; `usage.output_tokens` remains authoritative.

Thinking stringо‚љ

Type ThinkingDeltaо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaThinkingTurns struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Type ThinkingTurnsо‚љ

Value int64о‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaTokenTaskBudget struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

User-configurable total token budget across contexts.

Total int64о‚љ

Total token budget across all contexts in the session.

Type Tokensо‚љ

The budget type. Currently only 'tokens' is supported.

Remaining int64о‚љOptional

Remaining tokens in the budget. Use this to track usage across contexts when implementing compaction client-side. Defaults to total if not provided.

оЂЄ

type BetaTool struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

InputSchema BetaToolInputSchemaо‚љ

[JSON schema](https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12) for this tool's input.

This defines the shape of the `input` that your tool accepts and that the model will produce.

Type Objectо‚љ

Properties map[string, any]о‚љOptional

Required []stringо‚љOptional

оЂЄ

Name stringо‚љ

Name of the tool.

This is how the tool will be called by the model and in `tool_use` blocks.

maxLength128

minLength1

оЂЄ

AllowedCallers []stringо‚љOptional

One of the following:

const BetaToolAllowedCallerDirect BetaToolAllowedCaller = "direct"о‚љ

const BetaToolAllowedCallerCodeExecution20250825 BetaToolAllowedCaller = "code\_execution\_20250825"о‚љ

const BetaToolAllowedCallerCodeExecution20260120 BetaToolAllowedCaller = "code\_execution\_20260120"о‚љ

оЂЄ

CacheControl [BetaCacheControlEphemeral](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

Type Ephemeralо‚љ

оЂЄ

TTL BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLо‚љOptional

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL5m BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "5m"о‚љ

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL1h BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "1h"о‚љ

DeferLoading boolо‚љOptional

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

оЂЄ

Description stringо‚љOptional

Description of what this tool does.

Tool descriptions should be as detailed as possible. The more information that the model has about what the tool is and how to use it, the better it will perform. You can use natural language descriptions to reinforce important aspects of the tool input JSON schema.

EagerInputStreaming boolо‚љOptional

Enable eager input streaming for this tool. When true, tool input parameters will be streamed incrementally as they are generated, and types will be inferred on-the-fly rather than buffering the full JSON output. When false, streaming is disabled for this tool even if the fine-grained-tool-streaming beta is active. When null (default), uses the default behavior based on beta headers.

InputExamples []map[string, any]о‚љOptional

Strict boolо‚љOptional

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

Type BetaToolTypeо‚љOptional

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocationParamResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationWebSearchResultLocationParamResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocationParamResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocationParamResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

- `1h`: 1 hour

One of the following:

type BetaTool struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

InputSchema BetaToolInputSchemaо‚љ

[JSON schema](https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12) for this tool's input.

This defines the shape of the `input` that your tool accepts and that the model will produce.

Type Objectо‚љ

Properties map[string, any]о‚љOptional

Required []stringо‚љOptional

оЂЄ

Name stringо‚љ

Name of the tool.

This is how the tool will be called by the model and in `tool_use` blocks.

maxLength128

minLength1

оЂЄ

AllowedCallers []stringо‚љOptional

One of the following:

const BetaToolAllowedCallerDirect BetaToolAllowedCaller = "direct"о‚љ

const BetaToolAllowedCallerCodeExecution20250825 BetaToolAllowedCaller = "code\_execution\_20250825"о‚љ

const BetaToolAllowedCallerCodeExecution20260120 BetaToolAllowedCaller = "code\_execution\_20260120"о‚љ

оЂЄ

CacheControl [BetaCacheControlEphemeral](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

Type Ephemeralо‚љ

оЂЄ

TTL BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLо‚љOptional

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL5m BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "5m"о‚љ

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL1h BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "1h"о‚љ

DeferLoading boolо‚љOptional

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

оЂЄ

Description stringо‚љOptional

Description of what this tool does.

Tool descriptions should be as detailed as possible. The more information that the model has about what the tool is and how to use it, the better it will perform. You can use natural language descriptions to reinforce important aspects of the tool input JSON schema.

EagerInputStreaming boolо‚љOptional

Enable eager input streaming for this tool. When true, tool input parameters will be streamed incrementally as they are generated, and types will be inferred on-the-fly rather than buffering the full JSON output. When false, streaming is disabled for this tool even if the fine-grained-tool-streaming beta is active. When null (default), uses the default behavior based on beta headers.

InputExamples []map[string, any]о‚љOptional

Strict boolо‚љOptional

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

Type BetaToolTypeо‚љOptional

оЂЄ

CacheControl [BetaCacheControlEphemeral](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

Type Ephemeralо‚љ

оЂЄ

TTL BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLо‚љOptional

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL5m BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "5m"о‚љ

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL1h BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "1h"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Caching [BetaCacheControlEphemeral](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Caching for the advisor's own prompt. When set, each advisor call writes a cache entry at the given TTL so subsequent calls in the same conversation read the stable prefix. When omitted, the advisor prompt is not cached.

Type Ephemeralо‚љ

оЂЄ

TTL BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLо‚љOptional

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL5m BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "5m"о‚љ

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL1h BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "1h"о‚љ

DeferLoading boolо‚љOptional

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

MaxTokens int64о‚љOptional

Bounds the advisor's total output (thinking + text) per call. When the advisor hits this cap, the returned advisor\_result or advisor\_redacted\_result block carries stop\_reason='max\_tokens', and a truncation note is appended to the advice text the worker model sees (inside the encrypted blob in redacted mode). When set, the server also emits a remaining-tokens budget block in the advisor's prompt so the advisor self-shapes toward the cap. When omitted, the advisor model's default output cap applies and no budget block is emitted.

MaxUses int64о‚љOptional

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

Strict boolо‚љOptional

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

Iterations [BetaIterationsUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

OutputTokensDetails [BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

ThinkingTokens int64о‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оЂЄ

The number of web search tool requests.

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocationParamResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationWebSearchResultLocationParamResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocationParamResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Citations [BetaCitationsConfigParamResp](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Citations configuration for fetched documents. Citations are disabled by default.

Enabled boolо‚љOptional

DeferLoading boolо‚љOptional

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

MaxContentTokens int64о‚љOptional

Maximum number of tokens used by including web page text content in the context. The limit is approximate and does not apply to binary content such as PDFs.

MaxUses int64о‚љOptional

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

Strict boolо‚љOptional

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

BlockedDomains []stringо‚љOptional

оЂЄ

CacheControl [BetaCacheControlEphemeral](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

Type Ephemeralо‚љ

оЂЄ

TTL BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLо‚љOptional

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL5m BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "5m"о‚љ

Citations [BetaCitationsConfigParamResp](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Citations configuration for fetched documents. Citations are disabled by default.

Enabled boolо‚љOptional

DeferLoading boolо‚љOptional

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

MaxContentTokens int64о‚љOptional

Maximum number of tokens used by including web page text content in the context. The limit is approximate and does not apply to binary content such as PDFs.

MaxUses int64о‚љOptional

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

Strict boolо‚љOptional

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

UseCache boolо‚љOptional

Whether to use cached content. Set to false to bypass the cache and fetch fresh content. Only set to false when the user explicitly requests fresh content or when fetching rapidly-changing sources.

AllowedDomains []stringо‚љOptional

If provided, only these domains will be included in results. Cannot be used alongside `blocked_domains`.

BlockedDomains []stringо‚љOptional

If provided, these domains will never appear in results. Cannot be used alongside `allowed_domains`.

оЂЄ

CacheControl [BetaCacheControlEphemeral](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

Type Ephemeralо‚љ

TTL BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLо‚љOptional

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL5m BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "5m"о‚љ

const BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTLTTL1h BetaCacheControlEphemeralTTL = "1h"о‚љ

DeferLoading boolо‚љOptional

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

MaxUses int64о‚љOptional

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

Strict boolо‚љOptional

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

UserLocation [BetaUserLocation](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

City stringо‚љOptional

The city of the user.

Region stringо‚љOptional

The region of the user.

Timezone stringо‚љOptional

The [IANA timezone](https://nodatime.org/TimeZones) of the user.

#### BetaMessagesBatches

##### [Create a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/create.md)

client.Beta.Messages.Batches.New(ctx, params) (\*[BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/messages/batches

##### [Retrieve a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/retrieve.md)

client.Beta.Messages.Batches.Get(ctx, messageBatchID, query) (\*[BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md), error)

GET/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}

##### [List Message Batches](api/beta/messages/batches/list.md)

client.Beta.Messages.Batches.List(ctx, params) (\*Page[[BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md)], error)

GET/v1/messages/batches

##### [Cancel a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/cancel.md)

client.Beta.Messages.Batches.Cancel(ctx, messageBatchID, body) (\*[BetaMessageBatch](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}/cancel

##### [Delete a Message Batch](api/beta/messages/batches/delete.md)

client.Beta.Messages.Batches.Delete(ctx, messageBatchID, body) (\*[BetaDeletedMessageBatch](api/beta.md), error)

DELETE/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}

##### [Retrieve Message Batch results](api/beta/messages/batches/results.md)

client.Beta.Messages.Batches.Results(ctx, messageBatchID, query) (\*[BetaMessageBatchIndividualResponse](api/beta.md), error)

GET/v1/messages/batches/{message\_batch\_id}/results

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

оЂЄ

type BetaDeletedMessageBatch struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

ID stringо‚љ

ID of the Message Batch.

оЂЄ

Type MessageBatchDeletedо‚љ

Deleted object type.

For Message Batches, this is always `"message_batch_deleted"`.

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageBatch struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

ID stringо‚љ

Unique object identifier.

The format and length of IDs may change over time.

ArchivedAt Timeо‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which the Message Batch was archived and its results became unavailable.

CancelInitiatedAt Timeо‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which cancellation was initiated for the Message Batch. Specified only if cancellation was initiated.

CreatedAt Timeо‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which the Message Batch was created.

оЂЄ

EndedAt Timeо‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which processing for the Message Batch ended. Specified only once processing ends.

Processing ends when every request in a Message Batch has either succeeded, errored, canceled, or expired.

formatdate-time

ExpiresAt Timeо‚љ

RFC 3339 datetime string representing the time at which the Message Batch will expire and end processing, which is 24 hours after creation.

оЂЄ

ProcessingStatus BetaMessageBatchProcessingStatusо‚љ

Processing status of the Message Batch.

One of the following:

const BetaMessageBatchProcessingStatusInProgress BetaMessageBatchProcessingStatus = "in\_progress"о‚љ

const BetaMessageBatchProcessingStatusCanceling BetaMessageBatchProcessingStatus = "canceling"о‚љ

const BetaMessageBatchProcessingStatusEnded BetaMessageBatchProcessingStatus = "ended"о‚љ

оЂЄ

RequestCounts [BetaMessageBatchRequestCounts](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Tallies requests within the Message Batch, categorized by their status.

Requests start as `processing` and move to one of the other statuses only once processing of the entire batch ends. The sum of all values always matches the total number of requests in the batch.

оЂЄ

Canceled int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have been canceled.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

Errored int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that encountered an error.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

Expired int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have expired.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

Processing int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that are processing.

оЂЄ

Succeeded int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have completed successfully.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

ResultsURL stringо‚љ

URL to a `.jsonl` file containing the results of the Message Batch requests. Specified only once processing ends.

Results in the file are not guaranteed to be in the same order as requests. Use the `custom_id` field to match results to requests.

оЂЄ

Type MessageBatchо‚љ

Object type.

For Message Batches, this is always `"message_batch"`.

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageBatchIndividualResponse struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

This is a single line in the response `.jsonl` file and does not represent the response as a whole.

оЂЄ

CustomID stringо‚љ

Developer-provided ID created for each request in a Message Batch. Useful for matching results to requests, as results may be given out of request order.

Must be unique for each request within the Message Batch.

оЂЄ

Result [BetaMessageBatchResultUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Processing result for this request.

Contains a Message output if processing was successful, an error response if processing failed, or the reason why processing was not attempted, such as cancellation or expiration.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageBatchSucceededResult struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

ID stringо‚љ

Unique object identifier.

The format and length of IDs may change over time.

оЂЄ

Container [BetaContainer](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Information about the container used in the request (for the code execution tool)

ID stringо‚љ

Identifier for the container used in this request

ExpiresAt Timeо‚љ

The time at which the container will expire.

оЂЄ

Skills [][BetaSkill](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Skills loaded in the container

SkillID stringо‚љ

Skill ID

оЂЄ

Type BetaSkillTypeо‚љ

Type of skill - either 'anthropic' (built-in) or 'custom' (user-defined)

One of the following:

const BetaSkillTypeAnthropic BetaSkillType = "anthropic"о‚љ

const BetaSkillTypeCustom BetaSkillType = "custom"о‚љ

Version stringо‚љ

Skill version or 'latest' for most recent version

оЂЄ

Content [][BetaContentBlockUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оЃ–

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оЃ–

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оЃ–

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaTextBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationCharLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndCharIndex int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartCharIndex int64о‚љ

Type CharLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationPageLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndPageNumber int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartPageNumber int64о‚љ

Type PageLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Text stringо‚љ

Type Textо‚љ
