type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

type BetaCompactionBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Content stringо‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

EncryptedContent stringо‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

Type Compactionо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

From [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

Role Assistantо‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

StopDetails [BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

Category BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryо‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryCyber BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "cyber"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryBio BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "bio"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryReasoningExtraction BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Explanation stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

FallbackCreditToken stringо‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

FallbackHasPrefillClaim boolо‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

RecommendedModel stringо‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

Type Refusalо‚љ

оЂЄ

StopReason [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

const BetaStopReasonEndTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "end\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonMaxTokens [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "max\_tokens"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonStopSequence [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "stop\_sequence"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonToolUse [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "tool\_use"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonPauseTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "pause\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonCompaction [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "compaction"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonRefusal [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "refusal"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonModelContextWindowExceeded [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

StopSequence stringо‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

Type Messageо‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

Usage [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InferenceGeo stringо‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

Iterations [BetaIterationsUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

OutputTokensDetails [BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

ThinkingTokens int64о‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оЂЄ

The number of server tool requests.

WebFetchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web fetch tool requests.

WebSearchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web search tool requests.

Canceled int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have been canceled.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

Errored int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that encountered an error.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

Expired int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have expired.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

Processing int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that are processing.

оЂЄ

Succeeded int64о‚љ

Number of requests in the Message Batch that have completed successfully.

This is zero until processing of the entire Message Batch has ended.

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

Content [][BetaContentBlockUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оЃ–

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оЃ–

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оЃ–

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaTextBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationCharLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndCharIndex int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartCharIndex int64о‚љ

Type CharLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationPageLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

Type PageLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Text stringо‚љ

Type Textо‚љ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type BetaCompactionBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Content stringо‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

EncryptedContent stringо‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

Type Compactionо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

From [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

Model Modelо‚љ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type Model stringо‚љ

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

Response envelope for request-level diagnostics. Present (possibly
null) whenever the caller supplied `diagnostics` on the request.

оЂЄ

CacheMissReason BetaDiagnosticsCacheMissReasonUnionо‚љ

Explains why the prompt cache could not fully reuse the prefix from the request identified by `diagnostics.previous_message_id`. `null` means diagnosis is still pending вЂ” the response was serialized before the background comparison completed.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaCacheMissModelChanged struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CacheMissedInputTokens int64о‚љ

Approximate number of input tokens that would have been read from cache had the prefix matched the previous request.

Type ModelChangedо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCacheMissSystemChanged struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Approximate number of input tokens that would have been read from cache had the prefix matched the previous request.

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

оЂЄ

type BetaCacheMissPreviousMessageNotFound struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

type BetaCacheMissUnavailable struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

Role Assistantо‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

StopDetails [BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

Category BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryо‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryCyber BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "cyber"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryBio BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "bio"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryReasoningExtraction BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Explanation stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

FallbackCreditToken stringо‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

FallbackHasPrefillClaim boolо‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

RecommendedModel stringо‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

Type Refusalо‚љ

оЂЄ

StopReason [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

const BetaStopReasonEndTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "end\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonMaxTokens [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "max\_tokens"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonStopSequence [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "stop\_sequence"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonToolUse [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "tool\_use"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonPauseTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "pause\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonCompaction [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "compaction"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonRefusal [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "refusal"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonModelContextWindowExceeded [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

StopSequence stringо‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

Type Messageо‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

Usage [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InferenceGeo stringо‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

Iterations [BetaIterationsUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

type BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

OutputTokensDetails [BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

ThinkingTokens int64о‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оЂЄ

ServerToolUse [BetaServerToolUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The number of server tool requests.

WebFetchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web fetch tool requests.

WebSearchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web search tool requests.

оЂЄ

One of the following:

const BetaUsageServiceTierPriority BetaUsageServiceTier = "priority"о‚љ

const BetaUsageServiceTierBatch BetaUsageServiceTier = "batch"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Speed BetaUsageSpeedо‚љ

The inference speed mode used for this request.

One of the following:

const BetaUsageSpeedStandard BetaUsageSpeed = "standard"о‚љ

const BetaUsageSpeedFast BetaUsageSpeed = "fast"о‚љ

Type Succeededо‚љ

Container [BetaContainer](api/beta.md)о‚љ

ExpiresAt Timeо‚љ

оЂЄ

Skills [][BetaSkill](api/beta.md)о‚љ

SkillID stringо‚љ

Skill ID

оЂЄ

Type BetaSkillTypeо‚љ

Type of skill - either 'anthropic' (built-in) or 'custom' (user-defined)

One of the following:

const BetaSkillTypeAnthropic BetaSkillType = "anthropic"о‚љ

const BetaSkillTypeCustom BetaSkillType = "custom"о‚љ

Version stringо‚љ

Skill version or 'latest' for most recent version

оЂЄ

Content [][BetaContentBlockUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оЃ–

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оЃ–

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оЃ–

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaTextBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

Citations [][BetaTextCitationUnion](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationCharLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndCharIndex int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartCharIndex int64о‚љ

Type CharLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationPageLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

EndPageNumber int64о‚љ

FileID stringо‚љ

StartPageNumber int64о‚љ

Type PageLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

CitedText stringо‚љ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type WebSearchResultLocationо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaCitationSearchResultLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

Source stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Title stringо‚љ

Type SearchResultLocationо‚љ

Text stringо‚љ

Type Textо‚љ

type BetaCitationContentBlockLocation struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

оЂЄ

CitedText stringо‚љ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

DocumentIndex int64о‚љ

DocumentTitle stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

EndBlockIndex int64о‚љ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

FileID stringо‚љ

StartBlockIndex int64о‚љ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Type ContentBlockLocationо‚љ

оЂЄ

EncryptedIndex stringо‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оЂЄ

SearchResultIndex int64о‚љ

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

type BetaCompactionBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

Content stringо‚љ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

EncryptedContent stringо‚љ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

Type Compactionо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackBlock struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn routed directly by the sticky decision has no such boundary
and carries no block вЂ” the signal for whether a fallback model served the
response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оЂЄ

From [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The model whose output ends at this point вЂ” the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оЂЄ

stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

Role Assistantо‚љ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оЂЄ

StopDetails [BetaRefusalStopDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Structured information about a refusal.

оЂЄ

Category BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryо‚љ

The policy category that triggered the refusal.

`null` when the refusal doesn't map to a named category.

One of the following:

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryCyber BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "cyber"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryBio BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "bio"о‚љ

const BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategoryReasoningExtraction BetaRefusalStopDetailsCategory = "reasoning\_extraction"о‚љ

оЂЄ

Explanation stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оЂЄ

FallbackCreditToken stringо‚љ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вЂ” which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вЂ” if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оЂЄ

FallbackHasPrefillClaim boolо‚љ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вЂ”
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

RecommendedModel stringо‚љ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

Type Refusalо‚љ

оЂЄ

StopReason [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

const BetaStopReasonEndTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "end\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonMaxTokens [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "max\_tokens"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonStopSequence [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "stop\_sequence"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonToolUse [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "tool\_use"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonPauseTurn [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "pause\_turn"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonCompaction [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "compaction"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonRefusal [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "refusal"о‚љ

const BetaStopReasonModelContextWindowExceeded [BetaStopReason](api/beta.md) = "model\_context\_window\_exceeded"о‚љ

оЂЄ

StopSequence stringо‚љ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оЂЄ

Type Messageо‚љ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оЂЄ

Usage [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InferenceGeo stringо‚љ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

Iterations [BetaIterationsUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaCacheCreation](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

CacheCreationInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оЂЄ

type BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оЂЄ

OutputTokensDetails [BetaOutputTokensDetails](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вЂ” for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оЂЄ

ThinkingTokens int64о‚љ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always в‰¤ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оЂЄ

ServerToolUse [BetaServerToolUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The number of server tool requests.

WebFetchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web fetch tool requests.

WebSearchRequests int64о‚љ

The number of web search tool requests.

Type Succeededо‚љ

#### BetaAgents

##### [Create Agent](api/beta/agents/create.md)

client.Beta.Agents.New(ctx, params) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/agents

##### [List Agents](api/beta/agents/list.md)

client.Beta.Agents.List(ctx, params) (\*PageCursor[[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md)], error)

GET/v1/agents

##### [Get Agent](api/beta/agents/retrieve.md)

client.Beta.Agents.Get(ctx, agentID, params) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md), error)

GET/v1/agents/{agent\_id}

##### [Update Agent](api/beta/agents/update.md)

client.Beta.Agents.Update(ctx, agentID, params) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/agents/{agent\_id}

##### [Archive Agent](api/beta/agents/archive.md)

client.Beta.Agents.Archive(ctx, agentID, body) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsAgent](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/agents/{agent\_id}/archive

type BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401BashInput struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Input payload for the `bash` tool of the
`agent_toolset_20260401` toolset. All fields are optional;
a normal invocation supplies `command`, while `restart=true`
(with no `command`) reboots the runner-side bash session.

Command stringо‚љOptional

Shell command to execute. Omit only when `restart` is true.

Restart boolо‚љOptional

When true, restart the persistent bash session instead of
running a command. Subsequent calls without `restart` will
run against the fresh session.

TimeoutMs int64о‚љOptional

Per-call timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to the
runner-wide tool timeout when omitted or zero.

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401EditInput struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Input payload for the `edit` tool. Performs a string
replacement in the named file; by default `old_string` must
occur exactly once.

FilePath stringо‚љ

Path of the file to edit.

NewString stringо‚љ

Replacement text.

OldString stringо‚љ

Substring to find and replace.

ReplaceAll boolо‚љOptional

When true, replace every occurrence of `old_string`
instead of requiring a unique match.

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401GlobInput struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Input payload for the `glob` tool. Returns paths matching a
doublestar glob pattern, newest first.

Pattern stringо‚љ

Doublestar glob pattern (e.g. `**/*.go`). Absolute patterns
are only permitted when the runner is configured to allow
them.

Path stringо‚љOptional

Optional directory root to search under. Defaults to the
runner's working directory.

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401GrepInput struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Input payload for the `grep` tool. Searches file contents for
a regular expression, returning matching lines.

Pattern stringо‚љ

Regular expression to search for.

Path stringо‚љOptional

Optional directory root to search under. Defaults to the
runner's working directory.

оЂЄ

Input payload for the `read` tool. Reads file contents

type BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParamsResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A custom tool that is executed by the API client rather than the agent. When the agent calls this tool, an `agent.custom_tool_use` event is emitted and the session goes idle, waiting for the client to provide the result via a `user.custom_tool_result` event.

Description of what the tool does, shown to the agent to help it decide when to use the tool. 1-1024 characters.

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

Best combination of speed and intelligence

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeHaiku4\_5\_20251001 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"о‚љ

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeOpus4\_5 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-opus-4-5"о‚љ

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeOpus4\_5\_20251101 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-opus-4-5-20251101"о‚љ

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeSonnet4\_5 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-sonnet-4-5"о‚љ

High-performance model for agents and coding

type BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentCoordinator struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Resolved coordinator topology with a concrete agent roster.

оЂЄ

Agents [][BetaManagedAgentsAgentReference](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads, each resolved to a specific version.

ID stringо‚љ

Type BetaManagedAgentsAgentReferenceTypeо‚љ

Version int64о‚љ

Type BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentCoordinatorTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentCoordinatorParamsResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

оЂЄ

Agents [][BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParamsUnionResp](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1вЂ“20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

One of the following:

stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaSelfHostedWork struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Work resource representing a unit of work in a self-hosted environment.

Work items are queued when sessions are created or when long-dormant sessions
receive new messages. The environment worker polls for work to execute in a
self-hosted sandbox.

ID stringо‚љ

Work identifier (e.g., 'work\_...')

AcknowledgedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when the work item was acknowledged and assigned to a self-hosted sandbox

CreatedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work was created

оЂЄ

Data [BetaSessionWorkData](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The actual work to be performed

ID stringо‚љ

Session identifier (e.g., 'session\_...')

Type Sessionо‚љ

Type of work data

EnvironmentID stringо‚љ

Environment identifier this work belongs to (e.g., `env_...`)

LatestHeartbeatAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent heartbeat

Metadata map[string, string]о‚љ

User-provided metadata key-value pairs associated with this work item

StartedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution started

оЂЄ

State BetaSelfHostedWorkStateо‚љ

Current state of the work item

One of the following:

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateQueued BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "queued"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateStarting BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "starting"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateActive BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "active"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateStopping BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "stopping"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateStopped BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "stopped"о‚љ

StopRequestedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when stop was requested

StoppedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution stopped

Type Workо‚љ

The type of object (always 'work')

оЂЄ

type BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponse struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Response after recording a heartbeat for a work item.

LastHeartbeat stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of the actual heartbeat from DB

LeaseExtended boolо‚љ

Whether the heartbeat succeeded in extending the lease

оЂЄ

State BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseStateо‚љ

Current state of the work item (active/stopping/stopped)

One of the following:

const BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseStateQueued BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseState = "queued"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseStateStarting BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseState = "starting"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseStateActive BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseState = "active"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseStateStopping BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseState = "stopping"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseStateStopped BetaSelfHostedWorkHeartbeatResponseState = "stopped"о‚љ

TTLSeconds int64о‚љ

Effective TTL applied to the lease

Type WorkHeartbeatо‚љ

The type of response

type BetaSelfHostedWorkListResponse struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Response when listing work items with cursor-based pagination.

оЂЄ

Data [][BetaSelfHostedWork](api/beta.md)о‚љ

List of work items

ID stringо‚љ

Work identifier (e.g., 'work\_...')

AcknowledgedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when the work item was acknowledged and assigned to a self-hosted sandbox

CreatedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work was created

оЂЄ

Data [BetaSessionWorkData](api/beta.md)о‚љ

The actual work to be performed

ID stringо‚љ

Session identifier (e.g., 'session\_...')

Type Sessionо‚љ

Type of work data

EnvironmentID stringо‚љ

Environment identifier this work belongs to (e.g., `env_...`)

LatestHeartbeatAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent heartbeat

Metadata map[string, string]о‚љ

User-provided metadata key-value pairs associated with this work item

StartedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution started

оЂЄ

State BetaSelfHostedWorkStateо‚љ

Current state of the work item

One of the following:

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateQueued BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "queued"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateStarting BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "starting"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateActive BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "active"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateStopping BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "stopping"о‚љ

const BetaSelfHostedWorkStateStopped BetaSelfHostedWorkState = "stopped"о‚љ

StopRequestedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when stop was requested

StoppedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution stopped

Type Workо‚љ

The type of object (always 'work')

NextPage stringо‚љ

Opaque cursor for fetching the next page of results

оЂЄ

type BetaSelfHostedWorkQueueStats struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Statistics about the work queue for an environment.

Uses Redis Stream consumer group metrics for O(1) queries.

Depth int64о‚љ

Number of work items waiting to be picked up (lag from consumer group)

OldestQueuedAt stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of oldest item in the work stream (includes both queued and pending items), null if stream empty

Pending int64о‚љ

Number of work items being processed (polled but not acknowledged)

Type WorkQueueStatsо‚љ

The type of object

WorkersPolling int64о‚љ

Number of workers that have polled for work in the last 30 seconds. Requires worker\_id to be sent with poll requests.

оЂЄ

type BetaSelfHostedWorkStopRequest struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Request to stop a work item.

Force boolо‚љOptional

If true, immediately stop work without graceful shutdown

оЂЄ

type BetaSelfHostedWorkUpdateRequest struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Request to update work item metadata.

Metadata map[string, string]о‚љ

Metadata patch. Set a key to a string to upsert it, or to null to delete it. Omit the field to preserve existing metadata.

оЂЄ

type BetaSessionWorkData struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Work data for session work items.

This resource type is used when work represents a session that needs to be executed
in a self-hosted environment.

ID stringо‚љ

Session identifier (e.g., 'session\_...')

Type Sessionо‚љ

Type of work data

#### BetaSessions

##### [Create Session](api/beta/sessions/create.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.New(ctx, params) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsSession](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/sessions

##### [List Sessions](api/beta/sessions/list.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.List(ctx, params) (\*PageCursor[[BetaManagedAgentsSession](api/beta.md)], error)

GET/v1/sessions

##### [Get Session](api/beta/sessions/retrieve.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.Get(ctx, sessionID, query) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsSession](api/beta.md), error)

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}

##### [Update Session](api/beta/sessions/update.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.Update(ctx, sessionID, params) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsSession](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}

##### [Delete Session](api/beta/sessions/delete.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.Delete(ctx, sessionID, body) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsDeletedSession](api/beta.md), error)

DELETE/v1/sessions/{session\_id}

##### [Archive Session](api/beta/sessions/archive.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.Archive(ctx, sessionID, body) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsSession](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/archive

type BetaManagedAgentsMultiagent struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Resolved coordinator topology with a concrete agent roster.

оЂЄ

Agents [][BetaManagedAgentsAgentReference](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads, each resolved to a specific version.

ID stringо‚љ

Type BetaManagedAgentsAgentReferenceTypeо‚љ

Version int64о‚љ

Type BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentParamsResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

оЂЄ

Agents [][BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParamsUnionResp](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1вЂ“20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

One of the following:

stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

Version int64о‚љOptional

type BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResource struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Evaluation state for a single outcome defined via a define\_outcome event.

CompletedAt Timeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Description stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce.

Explanation stringо‚љ

Grader's verdict text from the most recent evaluation. For satisfied, explains why criteria are met; for needs\_revision (intermediate), what's missing; for failed, why unrecoverable.

Iteration int64о‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle the outcome is currently on.

OutcomeID stringо‚љ

Server-generated outc\_ ID for this outcome.

Result stringо‚љ

Current evaluation state. `pending` before the agent begins work; `running` while producing or revising; `evaluating` while the grader scores; `satisfied`/`max_iterations_reached`/`failed`/`interrupted` are terminal.

Type BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResourceTypeо‚љ

OutcomeEvaluations [][BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResource](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Per-outcome evaluation state. One entry per define\_outcome event sent to the session.

CompletedAt Timeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Description stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce.

Explanation stringо‚љ

Grader's verdict text from the most recent evaluation. For satisfied, explains why criteria are met; for needs\_revision (intermediate), what's missing; for failed, why unrecoverable.

Iteration int64о‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle the outcome is currently on.

OutcomeID stringо‚љ

Server-generated outc\_ ID for this outcome.

Result stringо‚љ

Current evaluation state. `pending` before the agent begins work; `running` while producing or revising; `evaluating` while the grader scores; `satisfied`/`max_iterations_reached`/`failed`/`interrupted` are terminal.

Type BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResourceTypeо‚љ

type BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResource struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A memory store attached to an agent session.

MemoryStoreID stringо‚љ

The memory store ID (memstore\_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

Type BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

Access BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceAccessо‚љOptional

Access mode for an attached memory store.

One of the following:

const BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceAccessReadWrite BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceAccess = "read\_write"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceAccessReadOnly BetaManagedAgentsMemoryStoreResourceAccess = "read\_only"о‚љ

Description stringо‚љOptional

Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.

Instructions stringо‚љOptional

Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.

MountPath stringо‚љOptional

Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.

Name stringо‚љOptional

Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.

оЂЄ

Stats [BetaManagedAgentsSessionStats](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Timing statistics for a session.

ActiveSeconds float64о‚љOptional

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

DurationSeconds float64о‚љOptional

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

оЂЄ

Status BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatusо‚љ

SessionStatus enum

One of the following:

const BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatusRescheduling BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatus = "rescheduling"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatusRunning BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatus = "running"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatusIdle BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatus = "idle"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatusTerminated BetaManagedAgentsSessionStatus = "terminated"о‚љ

Title stringо‚љ

Type BetaManagedAgentsSessionTypeо‚љ

UpdatedAt Timeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

Usage [BetaManagedAgentsSessionUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Cumulative token usage for a session across all turns.

оЂЄ

CacheCreation [BetaManagedAgentsCacheCreationUsage](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Prompt-cache creation token usage broken down by cache lifetime.

Ephemeral1hInputTokens int64о‚љOptional

Tokens used to create 1-hour ephemeral cache entries.

Ephemeral5mInputTokens int64о‚љOptional

Tokens used to create 5-minute ephemeral cache entries.

CacheReadInputTokens int64о‚љOptional

Total tokens read from prompt cache.

InputTokens int64о‚љOptional

Total input tokens consumed across all turns.

OutputTokens int64о‚љOptional

Total output tokens generated across all turns.

VaultIDs []stringо‚љ

Vault IDs attached to the session at creation. Empty when no vaults were supplied.

DeploymentID stringо‚љOptional

Deployment ID when the session was created from a deployment reference. Null otherwise.

type BetaManagedAgentsModel stringо‚љ

The model that will power your agent.

One of the following:

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeFable5 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-fable-5"о‚љ

Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeOpus4\_8 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-opus-4-8"о‚љ

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeOpus4\_6 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-opus-4-6"о‚љ

Most intelligent model for building agents and coding

Best combination of speed and intelligence

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeHaiku4\_5 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-haiku-4-5"о‚љ

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeHaiku4\_5\_20251001 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"о‚љ

Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeOpus4\_5 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-opus-4-5"о‚љ

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeOpus4\_5\_20251101 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-opus-4-5-20251101"о‚љ

Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeSonnet4\_5 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-sonnet-4-5"о‚љ

High-performance model for agents and coding

const BetaManagedAgentsModelClaudeSonnet4\_5\_20250929 BetaManagedAgentsModel = "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"о‚љ

High-performance model for agents and coding

stringо‚љ

Speed BetaManagedAgentsModelConfigSpeedо‚љOptional

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

const BetaManagedAgentsModelConfigSpeedFast BetaManagedAgentsModelConfigSpeed = "fast"о‚љ

type BetaManagedAgentsSessionAgentUpdate struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Mid-session agent configuration update. Only `tools` and `mcp_servers` are updatable. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. To preserve existing entries, GET the session, modify the array, and POST it back.

оЂЄ

MCPServers [][BetaManagedAgentsURLMCPServerParamsResp](api/beta.md)о‚љOptional

Replacement MCP server list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

Name stringо‚љ

Unique name for this server, referenced by mcp\_toolset configurations. 1-255 characters.

Type BetaManagedAgentsURLMCPServerParamsTypeо‚љ

URL stringо‚љ

Endpoint URL for the MCP server.

оЂЄ

Tools []BetaManagedAgentsSessionAgentUpdateToolUnionо‚љOptional

Replacement tool list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

One of the following:

type BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParamsResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A custom tool that is executed by the API client rather than the agent. When the agent calls this tool, an `agent.custom_tool_use` event is emitted and the session goes idle, waiting for the client to provide the result via a `user.custom_tool_result` event.

Description stringо‚љ

Description of what the tool does, shown to the agent to help it decide when to use the tool. 1-1024 characters.

Properties map[string, any]о‚љOptional

Name stringо‚љ

Unique name for the tool. 1-128 characters; letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.

Type BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParamsTypeо‚љ

type BetaManagedAgentsSessionStats struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Timing statistics for a session.

ActiveSeconds float64о‚љOptional

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

DurationSeconds float64о‚љOptional

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

type BetaManagedAgentsSessionUpdatedEvent struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Emitted when an UpdateSession request changed at least one field. Carries only the fields that changed; absent fields were not part of the update. The new configuration applies from the next turn.

ID stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

ProcessedAt Timeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Type BetaManagedAgentsSessionUpdatedEventTypeо‚љ

#### BetaSessionsEvents

##### [List Events](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.Events.List(ctx, sessionID, params) (\*PageCursor[[BetaManagedAgentsSessionEventUnion](api/beta.md)], error)

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events

##### [Send Events](api/beta/sessions/events/send.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.Events.Send(ctx, sessionID, params) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsSendSessionEvents](api/beta.md), error)

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events

##### [Stream Events](api/beta/sessions/events/stream.md)

client.Beta.Sessions.Events.Stream(ctx, sessionID, query) (\*[BetaManagedAgentsStreamSessionEventsUnion](api/beta.md), error)

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events/stream

type BetaManagedAgentsAgentCustomToolUseEvent struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

ID stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

Input map[string, any]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

Name stringо‚љ

Name of the custom tool being called.

ProcessedAt Timeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Type BetaManagedAgentsAgentCustomToolUseEventTypeо‚љ

SessionThreadID stringо‚љOptional

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.

type BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEvent struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent invokes a tool provided by an MCP server.

ID stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

Input map[string, any]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

MCPServerName stringо‚љ

Name of the MCP server providing the tool.

Name stringо‚љ

Name of the MCP tool being used.

ProcessedAt Timeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Type BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

EvaluatedPermission BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionо‚љOptional

AgentEvaluatedPermission enum

One of the following:

const BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionAllow BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventEvaluatedPermission = "allow"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionAsk BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventEvaluatedPermission = "ask"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionDeny BetaManagedAgentsAgentMCPToolUseEventEvaluatedPermission = "deny"о‚љ

SessionThreadID stringо‚љOptional

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its permission request on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.tool_confirmation` event to route the approval back.

type BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEvent struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent invokes a built-in agent tool.

ID stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

Input map[string, any]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

Name stringо‚љ

Name of the agent tool being used.

ProcessedAt Timeо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Type BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

EvaluatedPermission BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionо‚љOptional

AgentEvaluatedPermission enum

One of the following:

const BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionAllow BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventEvaluatedPermission = "allow"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionAsk BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventEvaluatedPermission = "ask"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventEvaluatedPermissionDeny BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolUseEventEvaluatedPermission = "deny"о‚љ

SessionThreadID stringо‚љOptional

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its permission request on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.tool_confirmation` event to route the approval back.

type BetaManagedAgentsBillingError struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

The caller's organization or workspace cannot make model requests вЂ” out of credits or spend limit reached. Retrying with the same credentials will not succeed; the caller must resolve the billing state.

Message stringо‚љ

Human-readable error description.

оЂЄ

RetryStatus BetaManagedAgentsBillingErrorRetryStatusUnionо‚љ

What the client should do next in response to this error.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

Type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetryingTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

Type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhaustedTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminal struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

The session encountered a terminal error and will transition to `terminated` state.

Type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminalTypeо‚љ

Type BetaManagedAgentsBillingErrorTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsCredentialHostUnreachableError struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

ID of the affected credential.

Human-readable error description.

оЂЄ

RetryStatus BetaManagedAgentsCredentialHostUnreachableErrorRetryStatusUnionо‚љ

What the client should do next in response to this error.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

Type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetryingTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

Type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhaustedTypeо‚љ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminal struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

The session encountered a terminal error and will transition to `terminated` state.

Type BetaManagedAgentsCredentialHostUnreachableErrorTypeо‚љ

type BetaManagedAgentsSystemMessageEventParamsResp struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Privileged context for the accompanying turn and all subsequent turns, appended to the session's system context as a `role: "system"` turn rather than replacing the top-level system prompt. At most one per request: it must be the final event and immediately follow the `user.message`, `user.tool_result`, or `user.custom_tool_result` it accompanies. Only supported on models that accept mid-conversation system messages.

оЂЄ

Content [][BetaManagedAgentsSystemContentBlock](api/beta.md)о‚љ

System content blocks to append. Text-only.

Text stringо‚љ

The text content.

Type BetaManagedAgentsSystemContentBlockTypeо‚љ

Type BetaManagedAgentsSystemMessageEventParamsTypeо‚љ

type BetaManagedAgentsMCPAuthenticationFailedError struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

Authentication to an MCP server failed.

Name of the MCP server that failed authentication.

What the client should do next in response to this error.

One of the following:

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

Type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetryingTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsUserInterruptEvent struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

An interrupt event that pauses agent execution and returns control to the user.

ID stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

Type BetaManagedAgentsUserInterruptEventTypeо‚љ

ProcessedAt Timeо‚љOptional

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

SessionThreadID stringо‚љOptional

If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.

оЂЄ

type BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEvent struct{вЂ¦}о‚љ

A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.

ID stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

Result BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEventResultо‚љ

UserToolConfirmationResult enum

One of the following:

const BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEventResultAllow BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEventResult = "allow"о‚љ

const BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEventResultDeny BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEventResult = "deny"о‚љ

ToolUseID stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

Type BetaManagedAgentsUserToolConfirmationEventTypeо‚љ

DenyMessage stringо‚љOptional

Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.

ProcessedAt Timeо‚љOptional

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

SessionThreadID stringо‚љOptional

When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.

оЂЄ

Event sent by the client providing the result of a custom tool execution.

ID stringо‚љ

CustomToolUseID stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.custom_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

Type BetaManagedAgentsUserCustomToolResultEventTypeо‚љ

оЂЄ

Content []BetaManagedAgentsUserCustomToolResultEventContentUnionо‚љOptional

The result content returned by the tool.

One of the following:
